scenario_name: Patchy emergence and partial replant creates overlapping crop attempts
operational_context: early-season establishment failure in patches; replant only in parts; sometimes with a different seed lot/variety.
key_objects: Field, CropCycle (parent + partial “child”), seed lot evidence, scouting evidence.
event_families: ObservationEvent (stand count), InterventionEvent (replant), StructureEvent (crop-cycle split/overlap relation).
commit_classes: observation assertion → operation claim; possibly governance decision if compliance/insurance depends on it.
pack_context: crop pack (maize/soy), insurance/subsidy pack, local “replant heuristics” pack; potential rule conflicts on “when is a new CropCycle”.
freshness_issues: stale “current crop cycle” if replant hasn’t been accepted/promoted yet.
outputs: Field PassportView; CropCycle PassportView; potential insurance dossier/submission.
why_hard: forces deterministic identity/lifecycle decisions (same vs new cycle) plus explicit overlap lineage.
baseline_fit: Partial — law strong, scenario fixture weak

scenario_name: Full crop failure → new crop planted (crop switch) within the same season
operational_context: crop fails (flood/hail/cold/heat) and farmer switches to another crop (or plants a cover crop) within the same field-year.
key_objects: Field, CropCycle A (failed), CropCycle B (new), Occurrence evidence (weather/damage), seed purchase evidence.
event_families: OccurrenceEvent (damage), GovernanceEvent (claim/decision), InterventionEvent (new planting), StructureEvent (cycle termination + successor).
commit_classes: occurrence/observation assertions; operation claims; compliance assertions where relevant.
pack_context: insurance/subsidy packs, regional agronomic packs; conflicting policies on “double cropping” or “practical to replant”.
freshness_issues: high risk if a submission assembles using stale “crop-in-force” state.
outputs: DossierAssembly (insurance); SubmissionAssembly (area/crop declaration update).
why_hard: multi-temporal truth (event vs effective dates) + severe downstream consequences if cycle lineage is wrong.
baseline_fit: Partial — law strong, multi-party filing fixture weak

scenario_name: Seed lot substitution mid-planting (mixed seed provenance inside one field)
operational_context: seed shortage or delivery delay; farmer switches seed lot or variety halfway through planting.
key_objects: AppliedResource (seed), CropCycle, ManagementZone (optional), evidence (tags, invoices).
commit_classes: operation claim + evidence record + later observation assertion.
pack_context: certification (non-GMO/identity preserved) pack may require stronger evidence; crop-system pack may change phenology expectations.
freshness_issues: later evidence arrival (seed invoice scanned weeks later) should update provenance without rewriting record-time.
outputs: CropCycle PassportView; buyer-facing Lot PassportView if identity-preserved marketing applies.
why_hard: “one crop cycle” but heterogeneous inputs → OFARM needs scoped input attribution without creating spurious new cycles.
baseline_fit: Partial — evidence/provenance law strong, scoped input attribution thin

scenario_name: Field boundary correction during the season (subsidy + operations mismatch)
operational_context: LPIS/IACS update or farmer correction changes eligible area; operations already recorded on old geometry.
key_objects: Field identity revision; historical operations; subsidy declaration evidence.
event_families: StructureEvent (field revision), GovernanceEvent (submission / correction), InterventionEvent (operations tied to geometry).
commit_classes: structure assertion; governance decision; evidence record.
pack_context: “subsidy filing” pack may install stricter geometry constraints; scope conflict with farm’s operational mapping pack.
freshness_issues: materialisation invalidation trigger due to identity revision affecting scope interpretation.
outputs: SubmissionAssembly (area/crop filing) and Field PassportView for audit.
why_hard: tests OFARM’s “same field, new revision” semantics and downstream query equivalence across revisions.
baseline_fit: Strong in law, partial in executable fixtures

scenario_name: Relay/double cropping creates explicitly overlapping crop cycles
operational_context: small grains followed by soybean, or undersowing/relay cropping creating concurrent cycles on the same field.
key_objects: Field; CropCycle A and B with overlap relation; interventions; yield measures.
commit_classes: operation claims; observation assertions; possibly governance decision for insurance declarations.
pack_context: policies diverge strongly by region—some systems support double cropping in claims, some restrict; pack conflicts likely.
freshness_issues: “current crop” view becomes ambiguous unless queries specify cycle identity and evaluation time.
outputs: CropCycle PassportViews for both cycles; possible SubmissionAssembly for insurance and acreage reporting.
why_hard: forces explicit overlap modelling (not silent overwrite) and robust querying.
baseline_fit: Strong in law, partial in query/output fixtures

scenario_name: Variable-rate fertiliser with partial machine logs and manual top-ups
operational_context: VR prescription applied; operator stops to refill; some passes recorded, others lost; manual correction later.
key_objects: AppliedResource (fertiliser), Equipment, OperationRecord claims, machine task data, evidence (delivery note).
event_families: InterventionEvent (application), EvidenceEvent (task file import), ObservationEvent (crop response).
commit_classes: operation claim + evidence record; later correction/supersession.
pack_context: NVZ/nutrient pack requires planning + record; equipment pack binds ISOXML/EFDI; potential merge on evidence policy.
freshness_issues: “applied N to date” is high-consequence for compliance decisions; stale materialisation must block filings.
outputs: Field PassportView; compliance report; NVZ submission or inspection dossier.
why_hard: reconciling conflicting sources without letting projections become truth; strong need for evidence and supersession traces.
baseline_fit: Partial — event/evidence law strong, reconciliation fixture weak

scenario_name: Manure storage + closed periods + emergency spreading constraints
operational_context: storage nearly full; weather blocks spreading; closed period approaching; risk-map and buffer rules apply.
key_objects: Facility/storage, AppliedResource (manure), Field constraints, evidence (plans, maps).
commit_classes: structure assertion; operation claim; compliance assertion; governance decision (NC/corrective action).
pack_context: NVZ/law pack plus local “risk map” templates; high precedence constraints over method convenience.
freshness_issues: time-policy triggers (crossing closed period boundary) can invalidate pre-planned actions.
outputs: SubmissionAssembly (records), DossierAssembly (inspection), corrective-action report.
why_hard: time-sensitive compliance rules + multi-party evidence—classic “freshness is purpose-sensitive” test.
baseline_fit: Partial — freshness/authority law strong, domain packs/templates missing

scenario_name: PPP application record is required, but product identity is ambiguous (label vs local name)
operational_context: operator records “fungicide X” in notebook; later needs exact authorisation number / formulation; digital record expectations rise.
key_objects: AppliedResource (PPP), evidence (label photo, invoice), operation claim record.
commit_classes: operation claim + evidence record; later correction.
pack_context: jurisdiction/regulatory pack defines required fields; certification pack may add stricter evidence retention.
freshness_issues: late evidence arrival after inspection dossier assembly.
outputs: Field/CropCycle passport; inspection dossier; submission package.
why_hard: forces OFARM to support “evidence that exists but is low quality” and upgrade pathways without rewriting history.
baseline_fit: Partial — evidence law strong, vocabulary/low-quality upgrade path thin

scenario_name: Spray drift allegation affecting an organic neighbour
operational_context: neighbour claims drift; organic integrity at risk; investigation requires weather, operation details, buffer compliance, and dispute handling.
key_actors: conventional farmer; organic neighbour; advisor; certifier/inspector; possibly insurer.
key_objects: Field(s), OperationClaim, Occurrence evidence (wind), lab residues, boundary buffers.
event_families: OccurrenceEvent (drift incident), ObservationEvent (residue test), GovernanceEvent (dispute/decision), EvidenceEvent (lab report).
commit_classes: occurrence/observation assertions; compliance assertions; governance decisions.
pack_context: organic certification pack vs pesticide safety pack; likely pack conflict on evidence sufficiency and outputs.
freshness_issues: high-consequence decisions must recompute current state before attested outputs.
outputs: DossierAssembly (case), possibly a corrective-action report.
why_hard: multi-party authority, contested truth, and output taxonomy discipline (this is a dossier, not a “passport”).
risk_clusters: authority; evidence; output taxonomy; pack merge
baseline_fit: Partial — dossier/authority law strong, cross-farm dispute fixture weak

scenario_name: Irrigation event with sensor telemetry vs human report mismatch
operational_context: irrigation performed; telemetry says shorter duration; human claims full application; water restrictions or permits may require accurate records.
key_objects: Equipment, OperationClaim, sensor evidence, advisory outputs.
event_families: InterventionEvent (irrigation), EvidenceEvent (telemetry capture), GovernanceEvent (audit if regulated).
commit_classes: operation claim + evidence record; possibly governance decision.
pack_context: water-use compliance pack; equipment telemetry mapping pack; potential merge on evidence policy and validation rules.
freshness_issues: irrigation scheduling advice is tolerant of stale state; compliance is not—tests twin boundary + bridge rule.
outputs: advisory report vs compliance submission (must stay distinct).
why_hard: “machine-generated vs reported” reconciliation plus twin separation.
baseline_fit: Partial — twin boundary strong, telemetry contradiction fixture weak

scenario_name: Weak-signal disease suspicion → lab confirmation arrives late
operational_context: scout suspects rust/FHB; sends sample; corrective spray decision happens before lab return; lab report arrives after the spray.
key_objects: Observation evidence (photos), lab report, intervention operation, hypotheses.
event_families: ObservationEvent (scouting/sample), EvidenceEvent (lab certificate), InterventionEvent (spray), GovernanceEvent (if compliance claim depends on confirmation).
commit_classes: hypothesis assertion → observation assertion; evidence record; advisory output; operation claim.
pack_context: crop-protection pack may demand threshold logic; certification pack may constrain spray choices.
freshness_issues: “current disease state” is inherently uncertain; OFARM must allow competing hypotheses in Advisory Twin but block fake certainty in Compliance Twin.
outputs: advisory risk report; later inspection dossier might need the lab report linkage.
why_hard: contradictions & uncertainty discipline under real decision pressure.
baseline_fit: Strong in twin law, partial in late-lab workflow fixtures

scenario_name: Remote sensing shows “stress”; scout says “fine” (contradictory observations)
operational_context: EO/NDVI highlights a patch; field walk contradicts; later yield map supports one side.
key_objects: Observation events (EO), narrative observations, hypotheses, crop zones (ephemeral vs durable).
event_families: ObservationEvent; possibly StructureEvent if a zone becomes governed.
commit_classes: observation assertion; hypothesis assertion; advisory output.
pack_context: advisory pack introduces EO layers; must not silently create constitutional zones unless explicitly governed.
freshness_issues: EO snapshots are time-sensitive; stale imagery can mislead operations.
outputs: advisory prioritisation view; Field PassportView should clearly label source and time.
why_hard: durable-vs-ephemeral zone rule + query provenance constraints.
baseline_fit: Strong in advisory/ephemeral-zone law, partial in provenance-heavy views

scenario_name: Local microclimate frost/hail incident triggers damage claims
operational_context: damage is spatially uneven; loss adjuster/insurer needs documented timing, extent, and pre/post photos; operations afterwards (replant, salvage for forage) depend on claim rules.
key_objects: OccurrenceEvent evidence, crop-cycle status, salvage intervention.
commit_classes: occurrence assertion; evidence records; governance decision.
pack_context: insurance pack; microclimate pack; crop pack; potential decision-rule conflicts.
freshness_issues: governance decisions depend on as-of state at incident time, not “now”.
outputs: DossierAssembly (insurance).
why_hard: multi-temporal modelling + high-consequence outputs with required basis snapshots.
baseline_fit: Partial — as-of state law strong, insurer dossier fixture weak

scenario_name: Harvest at high moisture → safe storage period countdown
operational_context: corn comes off wet; may be held “a bit” in a truck, floor pile, or bin before drying; safe storage time decreases rapidly with higher moisture and temperature.
key_objects: CropCycle harvest action, Lot creation, Facility/StorageLocation, drying intervention, moisture evidence.
commit_classes: operation claim; evidence record; observation assertion.
pack_context: post-harvest handling pack; buyer contract pack (moisture specs and discounts).
freshness_issues: material state (moisture/temp) is perishable; stale state is dangerous for operational decisions.
outputs: Lot PassportView; buyer delivery report.
why_hard: OFARM must model “time-sensitive material condition” without collapsing it into a single static attribute.
baseline_fit: Weakest operational gap — freshness law strong, perishable condition modeling thin

scenario_name: Drying damage and overdrying vs safe storage constraints
operational_context: overdrying causes cracking/darkening/seed damage; underdrying raises mould/insect risk; dryer logs may be incomplete.
key_objects: Lot, Facility, drying equipment, quality tests.
commit_classes: operation claim; evidence records; observation assertions.
pack_context: quality grading pack; safety pack; potential evidence-policy strengthening for attested outputs.
freshness_issues: drying parameters are needed “as-of drying time”, not later edited guesses.
outputs: lot quality report; dispute dossier if rejected.
why_hard: requires rigorous evidence/provenance handling and correction without deleting history.
baseline_fit: Partial — evidence/provenance strong, dryer-log semantics thin

scenario_name: Bin sanitation failure → insects migrate from old residues into new grain
operational_context: guidance repeatedly warns that leftover grain/dust can seed infestations; “do not put new grain on top of old grain” is a common rule-of-thumb; clean bins and equipment weeks before harvest.
key_objects: Facility/StorageLocation, Lot, evidence of cleaning actions, monitoring observations.
commit_classes: operation claim; observation assertion; evidence record.
pack_context: organic pack may limit chemical options; pest-control pack adds rules; safety pack.
freshness_issues: repeated monitoring updates; state becomes stale quickly if not recalculated.
outputs: storage management report; organic inspection dossier if relevant.
why_hard: long-running maintenance campaign across multiple lots and containers—high query complexity and evidence volume.
baseline_fit: Partial — event model fits, long-running storage campaign fixtures weak

scenario_name: Aeration and temperature stratification creates hidden spoilage pockets
operational_context: aeration is used to cool grain; cool temperatures reduce insect/mould activity; without proper aeration, temperature differentials build.
key_objects: Facility, Lot, aeration equipment, temperature cables.
commit_classes: observation assertions; operation claims; evidence records.
pack_context: storage management pack; equipment telemetry pack.
freshness_issues: operational decisions rely on current readings; stale materialisation should be marked and policy-limited.
outputs: Facility PassportView and Lot PassportView.
why_hard: high-frequency observations, potentially offline, requiring robust sync and materialisation policies.
baseline_fit: Partial — materialization law fits, high-frequency telemetry/offline fixtures weak

scenario_name: Lot creation from harvest stream → split into bins → partial merge later
operational_context: one field harvested over days; grain placed into multiple bins; later blended for shipment; quality varies by load.
key_objects: Lot identities, StorageLocations, Container occupancy episodes, lineage relations.
commit_classes: operation claim; observation assertion; evidence record.
pack_context: traceability pack; buyer contract pack; potential view shaping for buyer vs internal.
freshness_issues: “what is in this bin now” is a governed current-state question—materialisation critical.
outputs: Lot PassportView; ReportAssembly for shipment lot composition.
why_hard: OFARM Lot lifecycle rules + container/occupancy semantics must be honoured, or traceability collapses.
baseline_fit: Partial to weak — lot law exists, bulk-campaign executable coverage thin

scenario_name: Grain delivery grading dispute (moisture/dockage/grade) with re-examination
operational_context: moisture meters differ; dockage and grade factors affect price; disputes can trigger formal re-check and written determinations.
key_objects: Lot, delivery tickets, official weigh/grade results, dispute case file.
commit_classes: evidence record; governance decision; observation assertion.
pack_context: buyer contract pack; grain standards pack; dossier templates.
freshness_issues: dispute is about “as delivered” state; later storage changes must not rewrite it.
outputs: DossierAssembly (dispute); buyer-facing report.
why_hard: multi-party authority + immutable outputs + strong trace-back to basis.
baseline_fit: Partial — governance/output law strong, grade-basis packs/dispute fixtures thin

scenario_name: Mycotoxin risk management across harvest/storage with buyer testing and rejection
operational_context: mycotoxins can arise pre- and post-harvest; post-harvest handling/drying/storage is critical; some buyers test for DON/aflatoxin and reject sublots over thresholds.
key_objects: Lot, lab certificates, storage records, diversion decision (feed vs food).
commit_classes: observation assertions; evidence record; governance decision.
pack_context: food-safety pack; buyer-spec pack; evidence-policy strengthening.
freshness_issues: test results are per-sublot/time; later merges must preserve which sublot was tested.
outputs: DossierAssembly (food safety); compliance submission if required.
why_hard: forces sublot semantics and identity discipline; easy place for weak implementations to “average away” danger.
baseline_fit: Partial to weak — sublot identity and claim propagation remain dangerous

scenario_name: Organic + conventional share equipment and storage (commingling prevention plan under stress)
operational_context: same auger/truck/bin used; cleaning is imperfect; paperwork exists but execution is messy.
key_objects: Facility, Container, Lot, cleaning interventions, commingling risk evidence, organic system plan records.
commit_classes: operation claim; evidence record; compliance assertion; governance decision.
pack_context: organic certification pack imposes segregation controls; local method pack may conflict with evidence requirements.
freshness_issues: after a commingling incident, prior “organic lot state” may become INVALID for compliance use.
outputs: Inspection dossier; corrected lot passport; possibly nonconformity + corrective action.
why_hard: simultaneously tests lots, facilities/containers, evidence sufficiency, and output taxonomy.
risk_clusters: identity/lifecycle; pack merge; evidence; output taxonomy
baseline_fit: Partial to weak — lot/evidence/output law fits, mixed-context segregation stress is severe
commonness: Messy but common on mixed farms

scenario_name: Temporary storage outside “normal facilities” (piles, bags, drums) under weather risk
operational_context: bumper harvest or bin shortage; temporary storage used; moisture ingress and contamination risk increase; practices vary by region.
key_objects: StorageLocation, Container, Lot, evidence of conditions.
event_families: MaterialEvent (move to temporary storage), OccurrenceEvent (rain ingress), ObservationEvent (moisture/condition).
commit_classes: observation assertion; evidence record; operation claim.
pack_context: post-harvest pack; organic pack may restrict allowable surfaces/containers.
freshness_issues: condition changes quickly; late observations can invalidate earlier “safe” state.
outputs: Facility/Lot passports; buyer report.
why_hard: stresses container identity vs occupant identity and “current-state is derivative and explainable” requirement.
baseline_fit: Partial — container/storage identity law strong, temporary-storage risk policies thin
commonness: Periodic but common under capacity stress

scenario_name: Contractor executes operation but provides late/partial records
operational_context: custom applicator plants/sprays/harvests; farmer receives an invoice, maybe an ISOXML file later; details missing (rate, product ID, time).
key_objects: OperationClaims, EvidenceRecords, DelegationGrant (if contractor is allowed to report).
commit_classes: operation claim; evidence record; governance decision if contested.
pack_context: integration pack for task data; compliance pack requiring record completeness; pack merge risk on evidence-policy rules.
freshness_issues: revocation or changed delegation can occur before contractor “finalises” reported execution.
outputs: Field passport; compliance dossier.
why_hard: this is the archetypal stress test for executable authority with delegation and revocation re-check at promotion time.
baseline_fit: Strong in authority law, partial in ingest/evidence-quality upgrade fixtures

scenario_name: Owner vs operator vs tenant disagreement over who can approve compliance submissions
operational_context: land is owned by Party A, farmed by Party B, compliance obligations shared; approvals and signatures are disputed.
key_objects: AuthorityGrants, RoleAssignments, RevocationDecisions, SubmissionAssembly drafts.
commit_classes: governance decision; compliance assertion; evidence record.
pack_context: certification pack defines who must attest; governance pack defines action-class requirements.
freshness_issues: long-running “draft submission” crosses revocation boundary; must re-evaluate authority at final promotion.
outputs: SubmissionAssembly with attestation trace.
why_hard: directly exercises AuthorityActionClass, default-deny, human-only attestation defaults, and decision traces.
baseline_fit: Strong in authority law, partial in multi-signatory/revocation permutations

scenario_name: Advisor-prepared filing that requires human approval and traceable AI assistance
operational_context: advisor (or AI tool) drafts nutrient plan, spray log, or organic system update; farmer must approve; AI may be used for assembly.
key_objects: draft assertions, AuthorizationDecisionTrace, AI-assisted action markers, DocumentAssembly drafts.
commit_classes: compliance assertion; governance decision; evidence record.
pack_context: authority pack with AI rules; evidence policy may require human approval for final submission.
freshness_issues: if underlying current-state materialisation is stale, final assembly must block.
outputs: SubmissionAssembly.
why_hard: stresses REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL outcome and “assistance is not authority borrowing”.
baseline_fit: Strong in authority law, partial in stale-state + AI-assisted filing chain

scenario_name: Buyer requests read-only access to a lot, then disputes identity and demands deeper history
operational_context: buyer contract needs traceability; buyer wants lot passport; later disputes attributes (e.g., “same lot?”) and requests more evidence.
key_objects: SharingGrants, Lot passport view, evidence bundles, lineage relations.
commit_classes: evidence record; governance decision.
pack_context: buyer “profile” for the Lot PassportView; data sovereignty boundary must prevent silent over-sharing.
freshness_issues: buyer requests “current lot state”; must be generated from a clear context snapshot and basis.
outputs: Lot PassportView; possibly a ReportAssembly for contractual delivery.
why_hard: stresses output taxonomy (passport vs report), scoped sharing, and query reproducibility under different recipient profiles.
baseline_fit: Strong in sharing law, partial in recipient-profile deep-history retrieval

scenario_name: Certifier inspection finds missing evidence, issues nonconformity, and requires corrective action
operational_context: inspection asks for spray logs, cleaning records, invoices; gaps exist; corrective actions required.
key_objects: InspectionCase, NonConformity, CorrectiveAction, evidence bundle.
commit_classes: compliance assertion; governance decision; evidence record.
pack_context: certification pack; evidence policy pack; document assembly shaping for dossier.
freshness_issues: dossier assembled from stale state is unacceptable; must snapshot basis.
outputs: DossierAssembly; later SubmissionAssembly for corrective-action completion.
why_hard: stresses evidence sufficiency gating + supersession after a dossier already circulated.
baseline_fit: Partial — evidence/dossier/submission law strong, corrective-action campaigns thin

scenario_name: Pack conflict—two same-precedence packs touch evidence policy or decision rules for the same operation
operational_context: certification pack and local/community pack both define evidence sufficiency or decision rules; overlap is not declared safely mergeable.
key_objects: PackActivationSet, PackMergeResolutionTrace, evidence policy modules, decision-rule modules.
commit_classes: governance decision; structure assertion.
pack_context: surface family = EVIDENCE_POLICY or DECISION_RULE; safe merge modes differ and may HARD_FAIL without declared legality.
freshness_issues: pack activation changes context snapshot and can invalidate current-state materialisations.
outputs: Pack conflict report; governance dossier.
why_hard: deterministic failure behaviour is required; “prompt the user” is explicitly discouraged.
baseline_fit: Strong — pack law and hard-fail posture are explicit and executable
commonness: Rare per pack pair, dangerous when it happens

scenario_name: Late evidence after a formal submission was assembled
operational_context: submission filed; later missing lab report / ticket appears; must supersede or append without invalidating audit trail.
key_objects: EvidenceRecord late arrival; SubmissionAssembly; ReviewDecision supersession; MaterializationSnapshot of what was relied upon.
event_families: EvidenceEvent (late receipt), GovernanceEvent (supersede), possibly StructureEvent if scope identity revisions involved.
commit_classes: evidence record; governance decision.
pack_context: submission shaping + evidence policy; stricter retention for attested outputs.
freshness_issues: correction should not rewrite prior “generated-at” basis; new submission version required.
outputs: new SubmissionAssembly version; trace to prior.
why_hard: large stressor for supersession law, basis snapshots, and externally-visible corrections.
baseline_fit: Strong in supersession law, partial in post-filing correction workflows
